input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML:**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Header>
        <RefreshView
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
            IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
        </RefreshView>
    </CollectionView.Header>

    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
            <Grid Padding="10">
                <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
            </Grid>
        </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
</CollectionView>
```

A simpler and more common pattern wraps the entire `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**
```csharp
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        IsRefreshing = true;
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelectedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.NavigateToAsync("DetailPage",
            new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Item", SelectedItem } });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| Property | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | One-way | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` | **TwoWay** | Tracks selection; reset to `null` after navigation |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` → `ItemSelectedCommand` | One-way | Fires navigation on tap |
| `RefreshView.Command` → `RefreshCommand` | One-way | Triggers data reload |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | TwoWay | Controls the spinner |

**Tips:**
- Wrap `CollectionView` inside `RefreshView` (second pattern above) — it's the standard approach.
- Always set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the same row can be tapped again.
- Set `SelectionMode="Single"` — the default is `None`, which disables selection entirely.
